title: Hardcoded enum string "running"/"active" in cli_image_create.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_image_create.rs
Lines: 147, 173, 196

**Current code:**
- Line 147: `Some("running")` — MachineState comparison
- Line 173: `assert_eq!(img.state, "active", ...)` — ImageState comparison
- Line 196: `Some("running")` — MachineState comparison

**Problem:** Test assertions use hardcoded string literals instead of typed `MachineState`/`ImageState` enum variants. Compile-time type safety is lost.

**Suggested fix:** Use `MachineState::Running` and `ImageState::Active` typed enum values in assertions. May require changing test struct field types from String to the enum types.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — tests should still pass.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced hardcoded strings with typed enums

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on Brand (vmapi-api)
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** VMAPI `Brand` is used in vmapi-cli as a value_enum argument (vmapi-cli/src/main.rs:63) but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli`
close_reason: Closed

title: HTTP 410 Gone handling bypasses Progenitor client with 70 lines of manual code
description:
File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 551-620

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns HTTP 410 Gone with a Machine body (not an error) for deleted machines. Progenitor treats 410 as an error and fails to parse. Workaround: 70 lines of manual HTTP handling that bypasses Progenitor entirely, including:
- Manual `get_machine()` method with direct reqwest usage
- Custom `GetMachineError` enum with `NotFound` variant
- Bypasses Progenitor response parsing

**Suggested fix:** Investigate Progenitor response override capabilities. May require upstream feature request for non-error 4xx body handling.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — deleted machine queries should still work.
close_reason: Fixed: wrap Progenitor call, catch InvalidResponsePayload for 410 recovery

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Fixed by wrapping Progenitor get_machine() and catching InvalidResponsePayload (which surfaces when 410 Gone body can't be deserialized as Error). Reduced from 70 lines of manual HTTP/auth code to a 24-line match on the Progenitor error. Also removed cloudapi-cli (dead code, superseded by triton-cli) and switched all CLI callers to use the typed wrapper.

title: Hardcoded enum string "stopped"/"running"/"deleted" in cli_manage_workflow.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_manage_workflow.rs
Lines: 370, 385, 404, 530

**Current code:**
- Line 370: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "stopped", ...)`
- Line 385: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "running", ...)`
- Line 404: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "running", ...)`
- Line 530: `assert_eq!(instance.state, "deleted", ...)`

**Problem:** Test assertions compare against hardcoded string literals instead of typed `MachineState` enum variants. If variant serde names change, tests silently pass with wrong comparisons.

**Suggested fix:** Change test struct `state` field from `String` to `MachineState`, or compare against `enum_to_display(MachineState::Running)` etc.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — tests should still pass with typed comparisons.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced hardcoded state strings with MachineState enum variants

**Problem:** `VmState` is used in vmapi-cli as a value_enum argument (vmapi-cli/src/main.rs:60, 86) but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli`

title: Hardcoded enum string "failed" in rbac/apply.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs, line 797

**Current code:**
- `status: "failed".to_string()` in `ApplyChangeResult`

**Problem:** The `status` field in `ApplyChangeResult` is a `String` that takes values "ok", "failed", "skipped". These should be a typed enum.

**Suggested fix:** Define an `ApplyStatus` enum:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum ApplyStatus {
    Ok,
    Failed,
    Skipped,
}
```
Then change `ApplyChangeResult.status` from `String` to `ApplyStatus`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Replaced hardcoded status strings with ApplyStatus enum

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on Brand (cloudapi-api)
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/common.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** CloudAPI `Brand` enum is used in CLI instance creation commands but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the canonical API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch for the generated `Brand2` copy.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list on `Brand` in cloudapi-api.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added clap::ValueEnum derive to Brand enum

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on VmState
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs:66
The VmState enum has no #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. It is deserialized from server responses and used extensively across the codebase.

If the server returns a new VM state, deserialization will fail. Follows the forward-compatibility pattern from changefeed.rs and MigrationState.

Suggested fix: Add Unknown catch-all variant with #[serde(other)]. Note: VmState has manual Display and FromStr impls (lines 91-127) that do exhaustive matching and must be updated to handle the new variant.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make lint
close_reason: Closed

title: Action dispatch enums missing Clone+Serialize derives
description:
Files:
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs — MachineAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs — ImageAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs — VolumeAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs — DiskAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/vmapi-api (VmAction): `Debug, Clone, Deserialize, JsonSchema` — missing Serialize

**Problem:** Action dispatch enums lack `Clone` and `Serialize` derives. While these are only used in request body deserialization (not CLI-facing), missing derives reduce testability and round-trip capability.

**Suggested fix:** Add `Clone, Serialize` to all action dispatch enums for consistency with other enum types.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api`
close_reason: Added Clone+Serialize derives to all action dispatch enums

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on MachineType
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq`
**Missing:** `clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** `MachineType` is used in CLI code (instance list filtering) but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the canonical API type. Currently relies on Progenitor build.rs patch in cloudapi-client for the generated copy, but the re-exported API type lacks it.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list on `MachineType` in the API crate.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`

title: Hardcoded "unknown" fallback strings in CLI output code
description:
Files:
- cli/cloudapi-cli/src/main.rs:288,309 — `.unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown".to_string())`
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:946,987 — same pattern
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/disk.rs:143 — `.unwrap_or("unknown")`
- cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:94 — same pattern

**Problem:** Multiple CLI files use hardcoded `"unknown"` as fallback for optional enum values. Inconsistent with the `Unknown` variants that already exist on state enums.

**Suggested fix:** Define `const UNKNOWN_DISPLAY: &str = "unknown"` in a shared output module (e.g., `cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs`), or better yet, leverage the `Unknown` enum variants directly by making the state fields non-optional where possible.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-cli`
close_reason: Added opt_enum_to_display helper and UNKNOWN_DISPLAY constant

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on ImageType
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs:36
The ImageType enum is used as a CLI value_enum argument at cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:90 but does not have clap::ValueEnum derived on the canonical API type.

The build.rs patch (cloudapi-client/build.rs:46) covers the Progenitor-generated type, but the canonical API type should also have the derive for consistency with all other CLI-argument enums (MachineState, ImageState, Brand, etc.).

Suggested fix: Add clap::ValueEnum to the derive list on ImageType.

Verification: make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make lint

title: CreateMachine legacy format transformation is 152 lines of manual code
description:
File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 1109-1260

**Problem:** Node.js CloudAPI expects tags/metadata as flattened `tag.KEY=VALUE` and `metadata.KEY=VALUE` fields, but Progenitor generates nested object structures. 152 lines of manual transformation code converts between formats, including:
- `transform_progenitor_request_to_legacy()` — extracts nested fields and flattens
- `transform_create_machine_to_legacy()` — manual field-by-field construction
- Two separate methods: `create_machine()` and `create_machine_from_progenitor()`

**Suggested fix:** Investigate Progenitor `with_conversion()` for custom serialization, or implement serde custom serializer on the request type to reduce manual code.

**Verification:** Integration tests against real CloudAPI to confirm tag/metadata round-trip.
close_reason: Removed unused create_machine (API type variant) and 110-line transform_create_machine_to_legacy; renamed create_machine_from_progenitor to create_machine

title: Missing ValueEnum derive on VolumeState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs

**Current derives:** `Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
**Missing:** `Clone, Copy, clap::ValueEnum`

**Problem:** `VolumeState` lacks `Clone` and `Copy` (required for `clap::ValueEnum`), and lacks `clap::ValueEnum` itself. Progenitor build.rs patches the generated copy, but the canonical API type is incomplete.

**Suggested fix:** Change derives to `Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq, clap::ValueEnum`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Closed

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on JobExecution
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/jobs.rs:65
The JobExecution enum has no #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. It is deserialized from server responses via the Job struct (line 119).

If the server returns a new execution state, deserialization will fail. Follows the forward-compatibility pattern from changefeed.rs.

Suggested fix: Add Unknown catch-all variant with #[serde(other)].

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make lint
close_reason: Added #[serde(other)] Unknown variant to JobExecution

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on MachineState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs:43
The MachineState enum has an Unknown variant but lacks #[serde(other)], so it only matches the literal string "unknown" and will not catch new states the server may add.

This enum is deserialized from server responses via the Machine struct (line 84). If the server returns a new state value, deserialization will fail instead of gracefully falling back to Unknown.

Suggested fix: Add #[serde(other)] to the Unknown variant at line 52.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make lint
close_reason: Added #[serde(other)] to MachineState::Unknown

title: Untyped NIC state field — Option<String> should be NicState enum
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/network.rs, line 228
CLI usage: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs, line 155

**Current code:**
- API: `pub state: Option<String>` in Nic struct
- CLI: `"state" => nic.state == value` — raw string comparison

**Problem:** NIC state has known finite values (provisioning, running, stopped) but is modeled as `Option<String>`, losing type safety. The CLI does unvalidated string comparisons.

**Suggested fix:** Create a `NicState` enum in cloudapi-api types:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum NicState {
    Provisioning,
    Running,
    Stopped,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```
Then change `Nic.state` to `Option<NicState>`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added NicState enum, updated Nic.state field type

**Problem:** `IssueSort` is used in bugview-cli as a value_enum argument but lacks `clap::ValueEnum` on the API type. Relies on Progenitor build.rs patch in bugview-client.

**Suggested fix:** Add `clap::ValueEnum` to the derive list.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=bugview-api && make package-build PACKAGE=bugview-cli`

title: Untyped disk state field — Option<String> should be DiskState enum
description:
CLI usage: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/disk.rs, line 143

**Current code:**
- `disk.state.as_deref().unwrap_or("unknown")` — raw string with hardcoded fallback

**Problem:** Disk state is `Option<String>` in the Progenitor-generated types but has known finite values. The CLI falls back to a hardcoded `"unknown"` string.

**Suggested fix:** Create a `DiskState` enum in cloudapi-api types (or reuse `SnapshotState` if the values overlap), then update the Disk struct to use it.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added DiskState enum to replace Option<String>

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on MigrationState (vmapi-api)
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/migrations.rs

**Current variants:** Begin, Estimate, Sync, Paused, Switch, Aborted, RolledBack, Successful, Failed, Running
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If VMAPI adds new migration states, deserialization will fail. CloudAPI MigrationState correctly has `#[serde(other)] Unknown` but the VMAPI counterpart does not.

**Suggested fix:** Add `#[serde(other)] Unknown` variant to `vmapi_api::MigrationState`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`
close_reason: Closed

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on VolumeState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs, lines 25-30

**Current variants:** Creating, Ready, Failed, Deleting
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If CloudAPI adds new volume states in the future, deserialization will fail with an error instead of gracefully falling back. Other state enums (MachineState, SnapshotState, MigrationState, MigrationPhase) correctly use the `#[serde(other)] Unknown` pattern.

**Suggested fix:**
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum VolumeState {
    Creating,
    Ready,
    Failed,
    Deleting,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`
close_reason: Closed

title: Error schema patching should use Progenitor with_patch instead of patched/ directory
description:
Files:
- openapi-manager/src/transforms.rs (post-generation transform)
- clients/internal/cloudapi-client/build.rs:14-16 (reads from patched/)
- openapi-specs/patched/cloudapi-api.json (patched spec)

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns errors as `{code, message, request_id?}` but Dropshot generates `{error_code, message, request_id}` (required). Current workaround maintains a separate `openapi-specs/patched/` directory with a post-generation transform in openapi-manager.

**Suggested fix:** Use `with_patch("Error", &error_patch)` in cloudapi-client build.rs to rename `error_code` → `code` and make `request_id` optional, then read from `openapi-specs/generated/` directly. Eliminates the patched/ directory entirely.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-client && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated spec = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched spec = actual Node.js wire format. The diff is valuable migration documentation. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Won't fix: keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched = actual Node.js wire format. The diff documents the migration gap. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

title: Hardcoded enum string "created" in cli_snapshots.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_snapshots.rs
Lines: 223, 279

**Current code:**
- Line 223: `assert_eq!(snap.state, "created")`
- Line 279: `assert_eq!(snap.state, "created")`

**Problem:** Test assertions compare against hardcoded `"created"` string instead of `SnapshotState::Created` typed enum variant.

**Suggested fix:** Use `SnapshotState::Created` in assertions. May require changing test struct `state` field from String to SnapshotState.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Fixed: state field now uses SnapshotState enum
